brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +61.8% since Feb 27 — Brent $114.90 (Mon intraday); +51% in March — steepest monthly surge on record; Trump threatens to 'obliterate' power plants/Kharg; Apr 6 deadline
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 30 (Mon intraday): Brent $114.90-$116+/bbl. March surge +51% — steepest monthly rise on record, surpassing 46% in Sep 1990 (Gulf War). Dubai physical at $126/bbl — gap reflects acute physical supply stress. Goldman: if Hormuz at 5% flow for 10 weeks, daily Brent could exceed 2008 record of $147. Trump threatens to 'completely obliterate' power plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, desalination. Apr 6 deadline. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +51.9% since Feb 27 — WTI $101 (Mon intraday); breached $100 for first time since 2022; +48% in March; Apr 6 Hormuz deadline
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 30 (Mon intraday): WTI $101-$103/bbl. WTI breached $100 for first time since July 2022. March surge +48%. Gas at $3.98/gal national average (AAA). Brent-WTI spread ~$14. Dow in correction. Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran infrastructure if deal not reached. Apr 6 Hormuz deadline. Islamabad quad summit outcome pending. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.00 / +33.6% since Feb 27 — $3.98 AAA avg — pennies below $4 — EIA: $4.096/gal week ending Mar 23 — CA $5.86 — Polymarket: $5 by month-end
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 29-30: $3.98/gal (+33.6% since war began). EIA weekly data: $4.096/gal for week ending Mar 23. Up $1.00 in one month — pennies below $4 psychological threshold. California: $5.86/gal (4 counties above $6). AAA forecasts $4.00 nationally 'in coming days.' Polymarket: $5/gal by month-end. Source: AAA / EIA.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -510 pts / -7.4% — S&P 6,369 (Fri close); Dow correction; VIX 31.05; Mon futures +0.7% on Powell; avg S&P member -17% from highs
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri close): 6,368.85, -1.67% — seven-month low. Fifth consecutive weekly decline — longest since Russia 2022. Dow 45,167 in correction (-10% from ATH). Nasdaq 20,948 in correction. Average S&P member down 17% from highs; average Nasdaq member down 31%. VIX 31.05. 10-Year at 4.44% (highest since Jul 2025). Mar 30 early trading: futures +0.7% following Powell remarks that inflation expectations remain 'well anchored.' Defense stocks NOT sustaining initial surge despite combat. XOM $170.99 (+41% YoY). Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNN.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations' — $23B arms sales bypass Congress — $200B supplemental — Houthi third front — Apr 6 deadline
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 31: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations' in Iran including Kharg Island raids. Trump admin bypassed Congress on $23B+ weapons sales to Gulf allies. $200B supplemental faces bipartisan resistance. 82nd Airborne deploying. Houthi third front active. Trump threatens to 'obliterate' infrastructure if no deal by Apr 6. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Penn Wharton / WaPo.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (31 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$36.8B — $200B supplemental + $839B FY2026 + $156B reconciliation = $1T+ direct military this year
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 31: ~$36.8B. Pentagon $200B supplemental at ~$1B/day — faces significant bipartisan resistance. If approved alongside $839B FY2026 defense budget and $156B reconciliation, direct military spending exceeds $1 trillion this year. Bessent: 'we have plenty of money' but rules out tax increases. Rubio: 'weeks, not months.' Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 11,000+ US targets — 92% of Iranian Navy's largest vessels destroyed — 66% missile/drone production — 850+ Tomahawks — Bandar Khamir + steel/cement plants struck
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 30): 11,000+ US targets struck, 92% of Iranian Navy's largest vessels destroyed, 66% of missile/drone/naval production facilities destroyed. 8,000+ combat flights. 850+ Tomahawks. 150+ Iranian vessels sunk. Day 31: Bandar Khamir port city struck (5+ killed); steel plants Isfahan/Ahvaz struck; Iran Univ. of Science and Tech damaged; Haftkel water reservoir destroyed. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~31,000+ through Day 31. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Axios / DoD.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 14 US KIA confirmed — 6 Kuwait drone attack + 1 Saudi strike + 6 KC-135 crash + 1 additional — 300+ WIA — 29 injured Prince Sultan AB — 82nd Airborne deploying
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 14 US KIA confirmed: 6 in Kuwait drone attack (Mar 1), 1 from injuries in Saudi strike (Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26), 6 in KC-135 crash in western Iraq (Mar 12, non-hostile), 1 additional. 300+ wounded per CENTCOM. 29 US soldiers injured (5 seriously) at Prince Sultan Air Base from Iranian missile/drone strike (Mar 27). Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations.' 82nd Airborne deploying ~2,000-3,000 paratroopers. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 300+ total per CENTCOM — 29 injured at Prince Sultan AB (5 seriously) — 50,000+ deployed — 7,000-8,000 more incoming — Pentagon planning ground ops
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 300+ WIA per CENTCOM. 29 US soldiers injured (5 seriously) at Prince Sultan Air Base from Iranian missile/drone strike (Mar 27). 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR. Incoming: 82nd Airborne ~2,000-3,000; 31st MEU ~2,500 (arrived); 11th MEU ~2,500 (in transit). Combined incoming ground forces 7,000-8,000. Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations' including Kharg Island raids. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes / WaPo.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Red Crescent: ~1,900 — HRANA: 3,389 (1,527 civilians, 228+ children) — 226 medical units damaged — 12 hospitals non-operational — 87,000+ structures damaged
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources. Iranian Red Crescent: ~1,900 killed, 20,000+ injured. HRANA (independent): 3,389 killed including 1,527 civilians and 228+ children. HRANA Day 30 only: 17 civilians + 3 military killed. Health Ministry: 226 medical/health units damaged, 12 hospitals completely non-operational. Red Crescent: 87,000+ non-military structures damaged, ~600 educational centers attacked, 120+ historical sites damaged. Estimated aggregate including military: ~7,000. Iran launched 'Janfada' volunteer recruitment campaign. Source: Iranian Red Crescent / HRANA / Health Ministry / OCHA.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,247+ killed — over 1.2M displaced (~quarter of population) — IDF: 800+ Hezbollah killed — 3 journalists + 9 paramedics killed — UNHCR: $61M needed for Lebanon
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and Hezbollah operations since March 2. 1,247+ killed per Lebanese MoH. Over 1.2 million displaced (roughly a quarter of the population). IDF claims 800+ Hezbollah operatives killed including hundreds from elite Radwan Force. 3 journalists killed in Jezzine (incl. Al Manar TV's Ali Shuaib). 9 paramedics killed Mar 28. 4 hospitals and 50+ primary healthcare centers closed. UNHCR requested $61 million for Lebanon alone. IDF advancing toward Litani River with 3+ divisions. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNHCR / WHO.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.4M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 1.2M+ (quarter of population) — 70,000+ Afghan refugees expelled — global humanitarian aid disrupted
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.4M+ total displaced. Iran: ~3.2 million internally displaced. Lebanon: over 1.2 million (~25% of population). 70,000+ Afghan refugees expelled from Iran. Global humanitarian aid disrupted: WFP food for Afghan children stuck, WHO medicine for Gaza held up, IRC supplies for Sudan stranded in Dubai. Dubai's International Humanitarian City effectively crippled. Shipping containers face $3,000 emergency surcharge. OCHA activated contingency plans across 7 countries. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / WaPo / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — ~400 vessels stranded in Gulf of Oman — ~20,000 seafarers waiting — insurance 5%+ of vessel value — Spain closes airspace to US planes
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~400 vessels stranded in Gulf of Oman with ~20,000 seafarers waiting. Insurance rates surged from 0.25-0.5% to 5%+ of vessel value per transit. Iran 'tollbooth' system charging ~$2M per transit in Chinese yuan. Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure 'among our options' — dual chokepoint scenario. Spain closed airspace to US planes involved in Iran attacks (Mar 30). Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's List / S&P Global.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 228+ children (Iran) + Lebanon 124 + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school: 100+ schoolchildren — UNHRC investigating — 600+ educational centers attacked
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 228+ children killed in Iran. Lebanon: 124 children. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Minab school (Feb 28): 100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12 killed — UN Human Rights Council examining. 600+ educational centers attacked in Iran per Red Crescent. WHO warned of 'toxic black rainfall' from oil infrastructure strikes. ICRC: conflict has 'upended the lives of millions.' Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / ICRC.
